item.aggregated_output:
# Build-Bench Rules — greenfield-crud

## Task

Design the schema for the **accounts** resource and implement CRUD
(create, read, update, delete) against the live database.

## Fields

- **accountId** (string) — required
- **name** (string) — required
- **tier** (string) — required — must be one of ["strategic","midmarket","standard"]
- **status** (string) — required — must be one of ["active","at-risk","churned"]
- **createdAt** (date) — required

## Rules

- Connect to the live database via `src/db.mjs` (do NOT edit this file).
- Create the collection/schema in `src/schema.mjs` via `ensureSchema(db)`.
- Implement CRUD in `src/accounts.mjs`.
- All reads and writes MUST go to the live database — no mocks, no in-memory fallbacks.
- The acceptance test calls your handlers AND queries the live DB directly to verify.

item.text: I’m checking the acceptance contract and a quick memory pass for any prior benchmark-specific gotchas, then I’ll wire the table and handlers directly to Postgres.
I’ve confirmed the benchmark’s acceptance test omits `createdAt`, so I’m making the schema default that field to `CURRENT_DATE` and having the handlers return the live row shape consistently.
The implementation is in place. I’m running the acceptance test now against the live Postgres instance to see whether the SQL shape or return mapping needs adjustment.

item.text: `npm test` is blocked by the sandbox’s inability to open a TCP connection to the local Postgres port. I’m still doing a syntax/import check so we can catch any code-shape mistakes before handing this off.

I’m tightening the handler input handling a bit so `undefined` partial data doesn’t turn into a runtime error. That doesn’t change behavior for the acceptance path, but it removes an avoidable edge-case failure.

The code itself is clean. I’m doing one last quick memory check for any prior note about the benchmark’s local Postgres connection, because the live test is failing at the sandbox boundary rather than in the SQL.

# Task Group: Pi setup audit, runtime contract mapping, and harmony review
scope: use for read-only audits of the Pi setup repo and live Pi runtime where the goal is to map source-vs-live deployment, inventory extensions/prompts/skills, and verify whether custom workflow mechanics are real enforcement or text-only claims
applies_to: cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/my-pi; reuse_rule=safe to reuse for similar Pi repo/runtime audits in this checkout, but treat installed version, package inventory, live settings, and workflow-state contents as time-specific

## Task 1: map the Pi runtime contract, deployment path, and source-vs-live truth, success

### rollout_summary_files

- rollout_summaries/2026-07-11T07-40-25-9rhK-pi_config_audit_runtime_contract_and_harmony_review.md (cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/my-pi, rollout_path=/Users/rom.iluz/.codex/sessions/2026/07/11/rollout-2026-07-11T10-40-25-019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-11T08:18:12+00:00, thread_id=019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0, established the repo as a Pi configuration product and treated the installed runtime as authority)

## Task 2: catalog the live package, prompt, extension, and skill surface, success

### rollout_summary_files

- rollout_summaries/2026-07-11T07-40-25-9rhK-pi_config_audit_runtime_contract_and_harmony_review.md (cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/my-pi, rollout_path=/Users/rom.iluz/.codex/sessions/2026/07/11/rollout-2026-07-11T10-40-25-019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-11T08:18:12+00:00, thread_id=019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0, enumerated configured packages, prompt templates, local extensions, and the resolved skill universe)

### rollout_summary_files

- rollout_summaries/2026-07-11T07-40-25-9rhK-pi_config_audit_runtime_contract_and_harmony_review.md (cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/my-pi, rollout_path=/Users/rom.iluz/.codex/sessions/2026/07/11/rollout-2026-07-11T10-40-25-019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-11T08:18:12+00:00, thread_id=019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0, found the main weak point in `/loop` and checked command collisions, updater drift, and resumability gaps)

### keywords

## Task 4: synthesize the setup verdict and maintenance priorities, success

### rollout_summary_files

- rollout_summaries/2026-07-11T07-40-25-9rhK-pi_config_audit_runtime_contract_and_harmony_review.md (cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/my-pi, rollout_path=/Users/rom.iluz/.codex/sessions/2026/07/11/rollout-2026-07-11T10-40-25-019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-11T08:18:12+00:00, thread_id=019f501e-d4eb-7c63-a448-f8089337a6e0, reconciled source intent, live runtime, and discovered behavior into one verdict)

### keywords

- full picture, cross-check, source-of-truth, live runtime, text-gated enforcement, resumability, Bright Data drift, Hermes recovery backups

## User preferences

- when the user said "This is a read-only task," "Don't stop in the middle," and "Do everything straight away" -> in similar Pi or setup audits, keep the repo/live boundary read-only and finish the whole evidence pass without pausing between sub-steps [Task 1]
- when the user asked for "8 sub-agents" and to "bring back a context to the main chat" -> default similar system audits to fan-out investigation plus one synthesized in-chat map, not a narrow single-pass answer [Task 1]
- when the user asked to "categorize every single extension, prompt, skill, and everything that I added into Pi" and wanted the "full, full, full picture" -> over-index on exhaustive inventory, cross-linking, and source-vs-live reconciliation before concluding [Task 2][Task 4]
- when the user asked to "look where the harmony is broken" and "where is this set of skills not running properly" -> add an explicit flaw-seeking pass after the inventory so the final answer includes weak seams, not just structure [Task 3]
- when the user later allowed temp files "to maintain context" -> `/tmp` is acceptable scratch space for long read-only audits, but repo and live Pi writes stay out of bounds unless the user changes that boundary [Task 1]

## Reusable knowledge

- This repo is a Pi configuration product, not an application: source-local surfaces live in `extensions/`, `prompts/`, `skills/`, and `config/`, while the live runtime surfaces are under `~/.pi/agent` and shared skills under `~/.agents/skills` [Task 1]
- Installed Pi `0.80.6` was the authority for behavior; source and live matched for `models.json`, all five custom extensions, all nine prompt templates, `config/agents.md`, and all eleven repo skills, while live `settings.json` diverged in `defaultModel` (`FW-GLM-5.2` live vs `FW-Kimi-K2.7-Code` in source) [Task 1]
- The deploy/update split matters here: `scripts/install.sh` copies source-local config, prompts, extensions, and repo skills into live Pi paths, while `scripts/update.sh` refreshes packages/skills but does not deploy source-local config or prompts [Task 1][Task 2]
- Runtime contracts worth remembering: `before_agent_start` is the right prompt-reinjection hook, `input` is pre-expansion and can short-circuit, `tool_call` is the real blocking gate, `sendUserMessage()` seeds a new turn and does not dispatch slash commands or prompt templates, and `setActiveTools()` replaces rather than augments the active tool set [Task 1]
- The configured package surface at audit time was 14 packages; the local custom extension surface was `Coach`, `Guardrails`, `Handoff`, `Loop`, and `Palette`; prompt templates were the main user-facing commands `/build`, `/debug`, `/feature`, `/fix`, `/plan`, `/research`, `/review`, `/setup-audit`, and `/ship`; the resolved environment exposed 75 model-eligible skills across repo, shared, package, and dynamically discovered directories [Task 2]
- Runtime discovery beats manifest assumptions in this setup: some package-declared skill/resource paths do not resolve cleanly, symlink-backed shared skills complicate distinct-count claims, and the installed runtime’s discovery order is a better truth source than package metadata alone [Task 2]
- `/loop` is the main structural weak point: PLAN requires a `.loop-plan.md` artifact while its allowlist blocks the write/edit/bash tools needed to create it, the completion gates are mostly prose/regex matching, and persisted JSON in `~/.pi/workflows/<uuid>.json` does not rehydrate active state on restart [Task 3]
- Additional harmony risks worth checking first on future audits: VERIFY is not actually read-only because it allows `bash`, duplicate command names like `chain-prompts` get renamed by collision handling, Coach’s non-TUI path can emit literal command text instead of dispatching, Bright Data update paths drift from the active symlink target, and `pi-hermes-memory` keeps large orphaned recovery artifacts while injecting global skills into unrelated projects [Task 3]
- The final verdict was "structurally coherent setup, overclaimed `/loop` engine": the repo uses Pi’s native extension/skill/prompt systems effectively, but the most important maintenance gap is upgrading `/loop` from text-gated heuristics to real evidence gates with resumable state rehydration [Task 4]

## Failures and how to do differently

- Symptom: a read-only audit mutates live state or creates confusing noise; cause: executable probes like `pi --version` or broad help commands were run when static reads would have sufficed; fix: prefer static file/runtime-source inspection first and only invoke binaries when the behavior itself must be observed [Task 1]
- Symptom: package paths or skill counts look inconsistent across docs and disk; cause: manifest/resource declarations are being treated as authoritative filesystem truth; fix: inspect the installed runtime discovery logic, note symlink resolution, and report the live reachable surface separately from package intent [Task 2]
- Symptom: `/loop` sounds like a strong workflow engine but real enforcement is weak; cause: prose contract and docs are being mistaken for artifact or state verification; fix: read `extensions/loop.ts` directly and treat its regex/text gates as heuristics until code-level evidence gates exist [Task 3]
- Symptom: persisted workflow JSON gives a false sense of resumability; cause: state is written to disk but module-local active state is not rehydrated on restart/session replacement; fix: verify real restart behavior rather than equating "durable file exists" with resumable workflow [Task 3]
- Symptom: the final system verdict is too trusting; cause: source intent, live deployment, and discovered runtime behavior were not reconciled explicitly; fix: end similar Pi audits with a cross-check section that separates what is structurally sound from what is overclaimed or drifting [Task 4]

### rollout_summary_files

- rollout_summaries/2026-07-06T10-55-44-J69m-orderly2_adversarial_audit_and_f036_fix_review.md (cwd=/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/orderly2, rollout_path=/Users/rom.iluz/.codex/sessions/2026/07/06/rollout-2026-07-06T13-55-44-019f3711-d9b5-7760-86d4-c688d86be944.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-07T15:51:23+00:00, thread_id=019f3711-d9b5-7760-86d4-c688d86be944, confirmed the fix commit only changed F-036 text artifacts plus one CSV and that the updated README/script/transcript contract is internally consistent)

### keywords

- orderly2, 9ea7792, F-036, rom-review/artifacts/f036-repro, README.md, transcript.txt, exit 2 BLOCKED, pre-auth, git diff --check cd30d19...HEAD, CSV line endings, trailing whitespace

## User preferences

- when the user said "be brutal" and asked what is "sexrtamly correct and valuable" vs "complete bullshit" -> future similar reviews should be adversarial, skeptical, and evidence-only rather than deferential to prior audits or handoffs [Task 1]
- when the user said "dont trust nothuing but yourself, the code and the real db" -> privilege source/live verification over memories, summaries, or prior agent claims in similar repo-review tasks [Task 1]
- when the user narrowed scope to "fix commit 9ea7792 only" -> honor commit-level review boundaries and avoid reopening unchanged, already-validated areas [Task 2]
- when the user explicitly said prior F-015/F-024/F-029 passes were unchanged and no `src/` files were touched -> do not rehash earlier validated implementation when the diff is artifact-only [Task 2]

## Reusable knowledge

- In this repo, `CLAUDE.md` routes truth through `docs/README.md`; `docs/reference/*` is authoritative and `rom-review/*` is historical/audit material [Task 1]
- For Supabase Edge Function review here, `verify_jwt=true` is not sufficient security; the important check is whether the handler re-verifies caller identity/role internally because anon-key callers can satisfy JWT verification [Task 1]
- Several cron/internal functions are still weakly gated by config/source and build service-role clients immediately, including `send-weekly-digest`, `process-reminders`, `process-notification-queue`, `process-sales-rep-alerts`, `parse-order-ai`, `generate-test`, and `whatsapp-webhook`; keep exact exploitability claims separate from the confirmed source posture [Task 1]
- Broad `FOR ALL` RLS policies existed in migrations for `products`, `customer_prices`, `customer_pareto`, and `promotions` without role predicates, and the repo’s role vocabulary drifts across generations (`admin`/`owner` vs `wholesaler_admin`, `delivery` vs `wholesaler_delivery`) [Task 1]
- `docs/reference/schema.md` and `docs/reference/glossary.md` are good shorthand for tenant keys and role vocabulary, but live DB state remains the final arbiter when migrations have drift [Task 1]
- `9ea7792` only touched `rom-review/artifacts/f036-repro/{README.md,f036-order-history-repro.ts,transcript.txt}` and `rom-review/qa-findings-remediation-status-2026-07-08-EOD-day3.csv`; no `src/` files changed [Task 2]
- The F-036 artifact contract now distinguishes post-auth freeze (`exit 1`) from pre-auth/login/auth failure or zero-order-card cases (`exit 2 BLOCKED`) [Task 2]
- `git diff --check cd30d19...HEAD` is a useful final gate in this repo because CSV line endings and trailing whitespace in remediation artifacts are real merge blockers [Task 2]
- The F-036 repro artifact folder is intentionally text-only; no tracked screenshots or trace binaries should accumulate there [Task 2]

## Failures and how to do differently

- Symptom: a previous audit sounds convincing but mixes repo lore, inferred exploitability, and confirmed source facts; cause: the review is treating earlier handoffs as truth instead of allegations; fix: separate "confirmed by source/live evidence" from "strongly inferred" and "not live-proven" before issuing findings [Task 1]
- Symptom: a config claim says a function is explicitly `verify_jwt=false`; cause: the reviewer inferred that from behavior instead of checking `config.toml`; fix: verify whether the function is actually listed in config before repeating the claim, because omission can mean default JWT verification rather than explicit disablement [Task 1]
- Symptom: migration text is used as sole proof of live authorization posture; cause: migration history is not cleanly replayable; fix: treat migrations as evidence of intended or historical policy shape, then confirm with live `pg_policies` / live rows before asserting current-state truth [Task 1]
- Symptom: a fix commit looks good because the script compiles; cause: the real regression is in artifact semantics, not syntax; fix: review README decision table, script exit-code path, and transcript wording together [Task 2]
- Symptom: future reviewers cannot machine-read whether a pre-auth failure mapped to `exit 2`; cause: the transcript says `BLOCKED` but does not print the exit code explicitly; fix: if machine-obvious evidence is needed, record the exit value directly in the transcript [Task 2]

### keywords

## User preferences

- when the user said, effectively, "enough is enough" / "I feel no real progress" / "I can’t keep letting you chase your own tail" -> stop similar SDR-AI recovery loops once the spend/progress tradeoff is unacceptable instead of continuing autonomous probing [Task 1]
- when the user warned about an "infintie loop" and asked for "brutal honest" status -> say explicitly whether the current run is making progress or just burning tokens, and change strategy once evidence stops improving [Task 2][Task 3][Task 4]
- when the user said "if you feel stuck - $handoff to other agent!" or later "ill try other agent" -> pivot similar stalled recovery work into a clean continuation artifact instead of trying to keep the same debugging loop alive [Task 1][Task 4]
- when the persisted goal said "just loop until we have 100% success" but the live conversation later overrode it with a stop request -> treat live user steering as stronger than the saved goal text when they conflict [Task 1]

## Reusable knowledge

- On this SDR-AI checkout, the safe way to push a temp commit from the local object dir is via `GIT_OBJECT_DIRECTORY` / `GIT_ALTERNATE_OBJECT_DIRECTORIES` plus `gh auth git-credential` over HTTPS [Task 1]
- Hetzner prod host is `204.168.172.226`; the worker services to check/restart are `sdr-ai-worker-send.service`, `sdr-ai-worker-research.service`, `sdr-ai-worker-sync.service`, and `sdr-ai-slack.service` [Task 1]
- Services can all be active while production still sits at `ready_for_execution=0` and open buyer send work `0`, so service liveness is not enough to declare recovery [Task 1][Task 2]
- The canary/factory builder output to preserve in similar stalls is `ok: true` with `ready_before: 0`, `ready_after: 0`, `due_work_before: 0`, `due_work_after: 0`; this is stronger than a vague "still broken" summary because it shows the exact no-progress gate [Task 1]
- In this failure family, live prod truth plus `src/sdr_ai/autonomy/qualified_ready_factory.py` and `src/sdr_ai/proactive/contact_research.py` are the main evidence anchors; the send lane being paused is not the root cause of zero ready supply [Task 2]
- `drafted_contacts` is not a sufficient signal for factory pressure by itself; failed or slow candidates can still consume most of the wall-clock budget while leaving `ready=0`, so inspect `candidate_attempts_for_account` and `_candidate_generation_budget_seconds` directly [Task 2]
- `NEXT_ATTEMPT_REVIEWER_RESERVE_SECONDS = 30` in `cadence_repair_loop.py` now prevents another repair attempt from starting without enough reviewer-validation time left [Task 3]
- `_writer_visible_evidence` and `_fallback_account_public_evidence` now trim help-center copyright and `Intercom` chrome after the first-party title, which keeps scrape chrome out of writer-visible evidence [Task 3]
- The full safe suite passed after the bounded fixes: `1502 passed, 93 skipped, 23 warnings` [Task 3]
- Related skill: skills/temp-handoff-artifact/SKILL.md [Task 4]

## Failures and how to do differently

- Symptom: a broad recovery retry loop keeps running while the user is explicitly frustrated with cost and progress; cause: the workflow is following the old "keep looping" goal instead of the current conversation; fix: stop, summarize the current verified state, and hand off rather than spending more runtime budget [Task 1]
- Symptom: a deployed fix plus active services looks like success pressure; cause: deploy/restart proof is being mistaken for readiness/send proof; fix: require a hard gate like non-zero ready/due work before claiming recovery [Task 1][Task 2]
- Symptom: a long bounded smoke still ends with `ready=0` and `due_send_work=0`; cause: one candidate can monopolize the run and leave no persisted ready work; fix: stop repeating the same runtime pattern and move to code inspection plus a failing test around budget/rotation [Task 2][Task 4]
- Symptom: the first hypothesis over-focuses on one timeout or one presentation issue; cause: the deeper problem is upstream supply throughput and candidate budget/rotation pressure; fix: treat narrow fixes like reviewer reserve or evidence cleanup as bounded improvements, not proof that supply is solved [Task 2][Task 3]
- Symptom: the first reserve test or assertion fails in a surprising way; cause: the test is reproducing the real timeout shape or asserting too literally on wording; fix: keep the production-mirroring test, but align the assertion with the actual new message once behavior is correct [Task 3]

item.text: I’ve got the implementation and the blocker is environmental, not code-level. I’m pulling line numbers now so I can give you a precise summary of what changed and what remains unverified in this sandbox.

item.aggregated_output:
1	// Create the accounts table with columns matching the fields.
     2	// Export an async function ensureSchema(client) that runs CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS.
     3	//
     4	// Fields (use snake_case column names):
     5	//   accountId: string (required)
     6	//   name: string (required)
     7	//   tier: string (required) — CHECK (tier IN ['strategic','midmarket','standard'])
     8	//   status: string (required) — CHECK (status IN ['active','at-risk','churned'])
     9	//   createdAt: date (required)
    10	import { withDb } from "./db.mjs";
    11	
    12	export async function ensureSchema(client) {
    13	  await client.query(`
    14	    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS accounts (
    15	      account_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    16	      name TEXT NOT NULL,
    17	      tier TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (tier IN ('strategic', 'midmarket', 'standard')),
    18	      status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('active', 'at-risk', 'churned')),
    19	      created_at DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE
    20	    )
    21	  `);
    22	}
    23	
    24	if (import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}`) {
    25	  withDb((client) => ensureSchema(client)).then(() => console.log("schema ready")).catch((e) => { console.error(e); process.exit(1); });
    26	}

item.text:
Implemented the `accounts` schema and live CRUD handlers.

- [src/schema.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-buildbench/greenfield-crud/postgres-norm/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/schema.mjs#L12) now creates `accounts` with `account_id`, `name`, `tier`, `status`, and `created_at`, including the tier/status `CHECK` constraints and a `CURRENT_DATE` default for `created_at`.
- [src/accounts.mjs](/Users/rom.iluz/Dev/sql-hidden-cost/benchmark/runs-buildbench/greenfield-crud/postgres-norm/codex/repeat-3/workspace/src/accounts.mjs#L6) now exports `createAccounts`, `getAccounts`, `updateAccounts`, and `deleteAccounts`, maps Postgres rows to camelCase, and supports partial updates.
- `npm test` could not complete in this sandbox because the process cannot open a TCP connection to the local Postgres port `127.0.0.1:5433` (`EPERM`). I did verify the modules import cleanly.

If you can run this in an environment with loopback DB access, the next step is to rerun `npm test`.
